title: Scorching heatwave continues across West Bengal | Tap to know more | Inshorts - Inshorts
full_text: West Bengal is facing rising heat and humidity, making conditions uncomfortable across districts. South Bengal temperatures are expected to stay between 36-37°C, while western parts may approach 40°C. Light showers are likely in some districts, including Kolkata at 37°C, but relief will be limited as the heat continues despite intermittent rain and monsoon still not fully advancing.
